examples.title: E-Waste Sorting Algorithm
examples.description: Basic algorithm for sorting e-waste components using magnetic and eddy current separation
examples.code:
PROCEDURE EWasteSorting(waste_stream)
BEGIN
 INPUT: Mixed e-waste materials
 
 // Step 1: Magnetic Separation for ferrous metals
 ferrous_materials ← MAGNETIC_SEPARATE(waste_stream)
 remaining_waste ← REMOVE(waste_stream, ferrous_materials)
 
 // Step 2: Eddy Current Separation for non-ferrous metals
 aluminum ← EDDY_CURRENT_SEPARATE(remaining_waste)
 non_metallic ← REMOVE(remaining_waste, aluminum)
 
 // Step 3: Density Separation for plastics and glass
 plastics ← DENSITY_SEPARATE(non_metallic, density_threshold=1.5)
 glass ← REMOVE(non_metallic, plastics)
 
 OUTPUT: Sorted material streams
 RETURN ferrous_materials, aluminum, plastics, glass
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm demonstrates the mechanical sorting process used in primary e-waste recycling. Magnetic separation extracts ferromagnetic materials (iron, steel), eddy current separation uses electromagnetic fields to repel non-ferrous metals like aluminum, and density separation separates lighter plastics from heavier glass materials.

examples.title: Heavy Metal Detection Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to detect and quantify heavy metals in e-waste samples
examples.code:
PROCEDURE DetectHeavyMetals(sample)
BEGIN
 heavy_metals ← []
 
 FOR each metal IN [Lead, Mercury, Cadmium, Chromium] DO
 concentration ← SPECTROSCOPIC_ANALYSIS(sample, metal)
 
 IF concentration > THRESHOLD[metal] THEN
 ADD heavy_metals ← {metal, concentration, hazard_level}
 ALERT monitoring_system(metal, concentration)
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Calculate overall toxicity score
 toxicity_score ← 0
 FOR each entry IN heavy_metals DO
 toxicity_score ← toxicity_score + (entry.concentration * entry.hazard_level)
 END FOR
 
 IF toxicity_score > TOXICITY_LIMIT THEN
 FLAG sample as hazardous
 NOTIFY disposal facility
 END IF
 
 RETURN heavy_metals, toxicity_score
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm simulates the process of detecting heavy metals in e-waste samples using spectroscopic analysis. It checks for specific heavy metals, compares concentrations against safety thresholds, calculates an overall toxicity score, and flags hazardous samples requiring special disposal.
